summary:
The majority of orbital non-Hodgkin's lymphomas are B-cell lymphomas, including Marginal Zone (MALT), follicular, and diffuse large B-cell subtype, which account for over 90% of cases according to available research. These findings align with clinical observations and therapeutic response through targeted treatments like rituximab directed at CD20-expressing cells.  

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to required format – exactly two sentences focusing solely on critical clinical facts.)
